sources.title: AI agent development spec v3.1
sources.local_conclusion: Use three context tiers: hot instructions, specialist skills/agents, cold references. Prefer hooks and validators for deterministic behavior.

sources.title: OpenAI Codex AGENTS.md guide
sources.local_conclusion: AGENTS.md is project guidance discovered by Codex with a 32 KiB default limit; keep it concise and route to deeper docs.

sources.title: OpenAI Codex skills
sources.local_conclusion: Repo skills live in .agents/skills; SKILL.md has name and description plus optional scripts, references, assets, and agents.

sources.title: OpenAI Codex hooks
sources.local_conclusion: Codex hooks use nested event matchers. PreToolUse can guard Bash, apply_patch/file edits, and MCP tool calls, but is not a complete enforcement boundary.

sources.title: OpenAI Codex subagents
sources.local_conclusion: Codex can spawn specialized agents in parallel and collect results. Use for parallel exploration/review and carefully scoped implementation.

sources.title: Zed external agents
sources.local_conclusion: Zed runs Codex CLI through ACP. Zed is the UI transport, not the source of Codex instruction semantics.

sources.title: Claude Code extension overview
sources.local_conclusion: Claude separates persistent context, skills, subagents, hooks, MCP, and plugins. Match the tool to the kind of context or enforcement needed.

sources.title: Claude Code skills
sources.local_conclusion: Claude skills load only when used; move repeated procedures and long reference material out of CLAUDE.md into skills.

sources.title: Claude Code hooks
sources.local_conclusion: Hooks are lifecycle automation with nested event/matcher/handler structure. Prefer command hooks for production enforcement.

sources.title: Claude Code custom subagents
sources.local_conclusion: Claude subagents are specialized workers with their own prompts, tools, permissions, and optional skills.

sources.title: GitHub Spec Kit
sources.local_conclusion: Use Spec -> Plan -> Tasks -> Implement artifacts when task intent and work graph need to be explicit and agent-agnostic.

sources.title: Managed local GitHub Spec Kit snapshot
sources.local_conclusion: Keep a reviewed stable Spec Kit snapshot in the template and deploy projects through the official CLI at the pinned manifest ref.

sources.title: Trail of Bits claude-code-config
sources.local_conclusion: Treat agent setup as a continuous improvement loop: add rules, hooks, or skills only after real failures or repeated workflows.

sources.title: HumanLayer - Writing a good CLAUDE.md
sources.local_conclusion: CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md is high leverage because it enters every session; keep it intentional and project-specific.

sources.title: Nx AI agent skills
sources.local_conclusion: Move procedural monorepo knowledge into skills and keep MCP focused on live external systems.

sources.title: Anthropic - Building effective agents
sources.local_conclusion: Start simple, use workflows before autonomous agents, add parallelization/orchestrator-workers/evaluator loops only when they measurably improve the task.

sources.title: Anthropic - Effective context engineering for AI agents
sources.local_conclusion: Treat context as finite infrastructure: curate, isolate, compact, and give agents structured notes instead of dumping everything into one prompt.

sources.title: Anthropic - How we built our multi-agent research system
sources.local_conclusion: Use lead-agent orchestration with parallel specialized workers for broad research, then aggregate in the parent with explicit stop conditions and source quality checks.

sources.title: Birgitta Boeckeler - Context Engineering for Coding Agents
sources.local_conclusion: Separate principles, reference docs, how-tos, rules, and tool-fed dynamic context; developer experience now depends heavily on context enrichment.

sources.title: Birgitta Boeckeler - Harness engineering for coding agent users
sources.local_conclusion: A coding-agent harness combines feedforward guidance and feedback sensors. Improve the harness after repeated failures instead of relying on longer prompts.

sources.title: Thoughtworks Technology Radar - Context engineering
sources.local_conclusion: Progressive context disclosure, structured external memory, subtask isolation, and just-in-time retrieval are now mainstream engineering practices.

sources.title: Vasilopoulos - Codified Context: Infrastructure for AI Agents in a Complex Codebase
sources.local_conclusion: Use a three-tier context system: hot-memory constitution, specialized agents, and cold-memory specification documents, validated across long-running sessions.

sources.title: Zhang et al. - Agentic Context Engineering
sources.local_conclusion: Context can evolve through generation, reflection, and curation, but systems need safeguards against brevity bias and context collapse.

sources.title: Steve Yegge - Introducing Beads
sources.local_conclusion: Use git-backed structured task graphs, dependencies, priorities, and compaction instead of decaying markdown plans for long agent work.

sources.title: Boris Tane - How I Use Claude Code
sources.local_conclusion: Use a research artifact, a real plan artifact, human annotations, and then uninterrupted execution with checks. The plan is the human-agent contract.

sources.title: Ian Bull - Sinks, Not Pipes
sources.local_conclusion: Agent-ready code favors deep modules, honest interfaces, discoverable boundaries, and contained side effects over hidden cascades.

sources.title: Ian Bull - Finding the Working Memory Cliff
sources.local_conclusion: Agents degrade when tracking too many independent items. Use tools, chunking, task decomposition, and external state instead of asking the model to remember everything.

sources.title: Ian Bull - Planning Is Becoming the Bottleneck
sources.local_conclusion: When code gets cheap, judgment, sequencing, integration, and deciding what not to build become the bottleneck.

sources.title: Ian Bull - Code Reviews Are the Wrong Abstraction
sources.local_conclusion: Review intent, impact, risk, reversibility, and shared understanding, not just diff style. Automated style checks should move left.

sources.title: Dex Horthy / HumanLayer - 12 Factor Agents
sources.local_conclusion: Production agents should own prompts, own context, expose control flow, keep state explicit, use small focused loops, and pause/resume cleanly.

sources.title: Vuong Ngo - Scaling AI-Assisted Development with Scaffolding
sources.local_conclusion: Scaffolding and generators beat repeated prose instructions for enforcing conventions across large monorepos.

sources.title: Boris Cherny - Claude Code customization tips summary
sources.local_conclusion: High-throughput agent work uses parallel sessions, custom agents, MCP/tools, hooks, permissions, status feedback, and deliberate model/effort selection.

sources.title: Spec Kit Agents: Context-Grounded Agentic Workflows
sources.local_conclusion: Spec-driven phases need repository-grounding hooks and validation hooks so agents do not produce context-blind specs, plans, or tasks.

sources.title: Dive into Claude Code: The Design Space of Today's and Future AI Agent Systems
sources.local_conclusion: Modern coding agents are mostly harness: permissions, compaction, extensibility, delegation, storage, and isolation around a tool-using loop.

sources.title: A Language for Describing Agentic LLM Contexts
sources.local_conclusion: Complex agents benefit from explicit descriptions of context structure and dynamics rather than informal, implicit prompt assembly.

sources.title: Efficient Agents: Building Effective Agents While Reducing Cost
sources.local_conclusion: Agent design must track efficiency-effectiveness tradeoffs. Use cheaper deterministic steps, smaller agents, and selective high-effort reasoning.
